title: Race-level rank and probability post-processing
summary: ROC AUC is rank-based, and pit-stop probability within a race lap is a constrained relative decision: only a few cars usually pit next lap.
rationale: ROC AUC is rank-based, and pit-stop probability within a race lap is a constrained relative decision: only a few cars usually pit next lap. Current submissions use raw probabilities without enforcing within-race strategic ordering.
implementation_hint: After model prediction, transform scores within each Race/LapNumber or Race/Stint/LapNumber group by blending raw probability with within-group percentile rank, tyre-life percentile, and predicted top-k compression. Tune blend weights on OOF predictions only.
expected_effect: May improve AUC by sharpening relative ordering among cars on the same strategic lap without needing calibrated probabilities.
risk: Over-aggressive compression can damage global ordering across races; tune lightly and compare raw-vs-rank blend on race-group folds.
